lesson.menu_title: You Should, You Could, You Might — Modal Verbs for HR Advice
lesson.menu_desc: Giving advice and weighing options with should, could, and might -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- You Should, You Could, You Might -- Modal Verbs
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Murielle Xavier | Aula 19 -- Modal Verbs

listenings.text: Hello. Let me share some advice on our staffing dilemma. First, you should not panic. It is advisable to look at all the options calmly. You could offer flexible hours, or you might introduce a mentoring program. Both are feasible and worthwhile. Of course, every option has a drawback, so you should weigh up the costs and benefits. I would be cautious about a quick decision. The likelihood of keeping our best people is much higher if we listen first. In short, you should act, but you should act carefully.

Good morning. Let me talk through our options for the new policy. We could introduce it immediately, but that might cause resistance. Alternatively, we could test it with one team first. That is a more cautious and feasible approach. The main downside is that it takes longer. However, the likelihood of success is higher, and the drawback is small. It is advisable to weigh up both paths. I think a tentative rollout is worthwhile. We should decide this week, but we should not rush the details. Thank you.
